vision_statement: Decifer will be a cloud-hosted, profitable, multi-user trading bot platform where friends and family connect their own broker accounts through a web interface and manage risk via personal settings. The path runs through a proven single-account paper trading system, then live trading, then cloud deployment, then multi-user scale.
current_stage_assessment: Decifer is in paper_trading_single_account stage with a functioning signal pipeline (9 dimensions, 6 agents, consensus threshold), order execution, risk management, and a 100% passing test suite at 1046 tests. The codebase is maturing rapidly — recent commits show critical capital-safety bug fixes, logging improvements, and governance tooling — but the bot has not yet demonstrated sustained paper-trading profitability, which is the exit criterion for this stage before live trading can be considered.
executive_summary: Decifer has a solid and growing foundation: 6 shipped features, a 9-dimension signal model, 1046 passing tests, and active work on signal quality improvements. The most important near-term task is proving the bot is actually profitable on paper before advancing any stage — without that evidence, all downstream work (live trading, cloud, multi-user) is premature. The active feature list is dangerously overloaded at 50 items, creating risk of scattered development effort; the WIP limit commit suggests the team is aware of this. The feature pipeline contains significant duplication (e.g., IC-Weighted Signal Scoring appears twice, Connection Manager appears in both Active and Backlog, multiple overlapping Reddit/sentiment items), which must be reconciled. The architecture for multi-account and multi-user is beginning to take shape but should not be the current focus — profitability and reliability at single-account paper trading must come first.

product_roadmap.goal: Demonstrate that the bot generates positive risk-adjusted returns on paper over a sustained period (minimum 60 trading days), with reliable execution and no critical bugs.
product_roadmap.rationale: This is the exit criterion for the current stage. Moving to live trading without paper profitability proof would expose real capital to an unvalidated system. Everything in later phases depends on this being true.
features: Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard — weekly IC rolling check per dimension to identify dead signals early
Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite — cross-sectional standardisation so no single dimension dominates by scale
Multi-Timeframe Signal Confirmation — daily/4H/1H alignment to reduce false entry signals
Regime-Gated Momentum / Mean-Reversion Signal Router — route signal logic based on VIX proxy regime state
Regime-Conditional Signal Weighting (Momentum vs Mean-Reversion Gate) — weight dimensions by regime
Options Flow Proxy Signal via Put/Call Ratio — free yfinance options chain, adds institutional sentiment layer
Unusual Options Activity (UOA) Signal Dimension — peer-reviewed alpha source, adds to signal model
ATR Volatility-Targeting Position Size Layer — replace or cap current sizing with volatility-normalised formula
Drawdown Brake — Tiered Equity Curve Circuit Breaker — automatic size reduction at drawdown tiers
Dual-Tranche Exit Strategy — split position into two tranches for partial profit-taking and trailing stop
Alpha Decay Chart — Per-Trade Forward Return Distribution Tracker — visualise whether signals are decaying
Walk-Forward Backtesting — rolling train/test windows to validate signal model without lookahead bias
Slippage & Execution Cost Model for Backtester — realistic cost model to avoid overstated backtest results
Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop — architectural prerequisite for clean signal routing and testing
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot shows positive Sharpe ratio (>0.5) and positive total return over minimum 60 paper trading days with no capital-safety bugs triggered. Walk-forward backtest validates signal model. Drawdown brake fires correctly in simulation.

product_roadmap.goal: Upgrade the signal model from equal-weight static to IC-weighted dynamic, add the highest-confidence new signal dimensions, and validate with Alphalens.
product_roadmap.rationale: Research clearly shows IC-weighted dynamic signal models substantially outperform equal-weight static ones. This upgrade should follow initial paper profitability proof so it improves an already-working baseline rather than replacing a system whose baseline performance is unknown.
features: IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scorer — replace equal-weight composite with rolling IC-based weights (deduplicated from two Active items into one)
Signal Correlation De-Duplication Guard — prevent correlated dimensions from double-counting exposure
Alpha Decay Monitor — Per-Signal IC Rolling Health Check — automated per-dimension IC tracking
SEC Form 4 Insider Cluster Buying Dimension — free EDGAR RSS feed, strong peer-validated alpha source
News Sentiment Regression Scorer (Yahoo RSS → FinBERT) — arXiv-validated sentiment regression signal
Claude Sentiment Scoring Pipeline — structured sentiment as 10th signal dimension
Meta-Labeling Secondary Classifier — binary classifier on top of primary signal to filter low-confidence trades
Triple Barrier Method for ML Training Labels — replace binary labels with triple barrier for ML training
Signal Validation with Alphalens — IC scores, turnover, and decay curves per dimension
Order Flow Imbalance Detection — institutional accumulation proxy from volume patterns
Time-of-Day Seasonality Filter — avoid low-alpha time windows using 60-day intraday OHLCV analysis
Anchored VWAP Entry Gate — binary gate to avoid entries on wrong side of anchored VWAP
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: IC-weighted model demonstrably outperforms equal-weight baseline in walk-forward test. All active signal dimensions pass Alphalens IC > 0.05 threshold. New dimensions integrated and validated.

product_roadmap.goal: Transition from paper to live trading on a single account with hard capital-safety guardrails, kill switches, and monitoring.
product_roadmap.rationale: Live trading introduces slippage, partial fills, broker errors, and real capital risk. All safety infrastructure must be in place before this transition. The Telegram kill switch commit shows this is already being thought about.
features: VIX-Rank Adaptive Fractional Kelly Sizer — dynamic Kelly fraction that shrinks in high-VIX environments
Monte Carlo Stress Testing — shuffle backtest sequences 1000x to validate true drawdown distribution before going live
Portfolio Heat Monitor with Correlation-Adjusted Exposure Cap — hard cap on total correlated exposure before any order
Correlation-Aware Position Management — 20-day rolling correlation matrix to prevent hidden concentration
Intra-Portfolio Rolling Correlation Guard with Sector Cap — 30-day pairwise correlation guard with sector-level cap
Multi-Timeframe Momentum Confirmation Filter — additional confirmation layer before live entry
Telegram Bot — Notifications and Quick Commands — live trade notifications and kill switch commands
Rolling CVaR Dashboard Metric and Per-Account Risk Budget — 30-day CVaR per account as risk budget gauge
Phase gate enforcement — hard coded gate requiring manual confirmation before any live order is submitted
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot has run 30+ days live with real capital, no capital-safety breaches, drawdown within expected parameters from Monte Carlo model, Telegram kill switch tested and confirmed functional.

product_roadmap.goal: Move the single-account bot from local to cloud VPS with Docker, persistent state, reconnection logic, and a web dashboard.
product_roadmap.rationale: Cloud hosting is a prerequisite for multi-user. It must be proven stable and reliable for the founder's own account before any other user is onboarded.
features: Cloud VPS Setup and Deployment — Ubuntu 22, Docker, shared brain container (deduplicated Active + Backlog item)
Reconnection Logic — Backoff, Sequence, State Sync — exponential backoff, state sync on reconnect
Per-Account Local Dashboard → Web Dashboard — Live Positions and P&L — browser-accessible dashboard
WebSocket Signal Broadcaster — brain publishes signals to connected containers in real time
Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop — if not completed in Phase 1, required here for container architecture
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Bot runs continuously on cloud VPS for 14+ days with zero unplanned downtime, survives at least one simulated network interruption with clean reconnect, web dashboard accessible and accurate.

product_roadmap.goal: Onboard a small group of trusted friends/family onto the platform with isolated accounts, per-user configuration, authentication, and dashboard access.
product_roadmap.rationale: Multi-user introduces account isolation, credential security, per-user risk profiles, and UI/UX requirements that don't exist in single-user mode. These must be built deliberately before any external user connects real money.
features: Per-Account Config Profiles — account-aware profile system replacing flat global CONFIG (deduplicated MA1 P0 + Active)
AccountDecisionAgent Class — per-account agent instance downstream of shared signal pipeline (deduplicated)
Connection Manager — Multi-Account IBKR — manages multiple IB() instances with unique ports (deduplicated)
Parameterise account_id Through Orders and Risk — audit all CONFIG['active_account'] reads and replace (deduplicated)
Per-User Docker Compose Containers — isolated container per user with encrypted credentials
Per-Account Risk Profile Config Schema with Multi-Tier Presets — RiskProfile dataclass with tiered presets
Per-User Dashboard Authentication — login, session management, per-user view isolation
One-Time User Setup Screen — guided IBKR credential entry and risk profile selection on first login
Signal Registry — Per-User Favourite Symbols — per-user symbol subscription registry in brain
Per-Account Risk Limits — independent max drawdown, position size, sector concentration per account
Account-Aware Order Router — route orders to correct IBKR account by strategy assignment
Multi-Account Position Aggregation — already shipped, integrate into multi-user dashboard
Cross-Account Correlation Guard — detect unintended correlated exposure across accounts
Unified Trade Journal — cross-account trade log with account tags
Account Performance Comparison — side-by-side Sharpe, drawdown, return dashboard
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: 3+ users running live with real capital, each with isolated containers and risk profiles, no cross-account data leakage confirmed, all users can access their own dashboard, Telegram notifications working per user.

product_roadmap.goal: Harden the platform for broader rollout: security audit, uptime SLA, advanced analytics, and regime-adaptive ML.
product_roadmap.rationale: Production readiness requires hardening that is impractical to build while also onboarding the first users. This phase addresses the remaining D-tier research features and operational excellence.
features: HMM Regime Detection — 3-state HMM on vol + RSI for regime-aware strategy adaptation
Regime-Conditioned Position Size Scalar — connect HMM output to position sizing
Walk-Forward Weight Calibration — systematic signal weight optimisation over rolling windows
Reddit Mention Velocity Dimension — quantitative WSB velocity signal if research validation supports it
Dynamic Volatility-Based Position Sizing — full ATR-based volatility ceiling on Kelly output
Security audit of credential handling and container isolation
Uptime monitoring and alerting infrastructure
User onboarding documentation
product_roadmap.exit_criteria: Platform running with 5+ users, 99%+ uptime over 30 days, security audit passed, all P0/P1 backlog items resolved.

immediate_next_action.action: Implement the Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard and Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite — the two highest-leverage Phase 1 items for proving paper trading profitability.
immediate_next_action.rationale: The bot cannot exit paper_trading_single_account without evidence of profitability. Before optimising signal weights or adding new dimensions, the team needs visibility into whether the current 9 dimensions have any predictive power at all (Signal Decay Monitor) and needs to ensure no single dimension is dominating the composite by scale rather than quality (Z-Score Normalisation). These two features together give the first honest read on signal model health and are prerequisites for any IC-weighting work. Both are P1 Active items with no blocking dependencies.
immediate_next_action.cowork_prompt: We are in paper_trading_single_account stage and need to prove the bot is profitable before advancing. The two highest-priority Phase 1 tasks are: (1) Signal Decay Monitor & Freshness Dashboard — a scheduled weekly process in learning.py or a new signal_health.py that computes rolling IC (information coefficient) for each of Decifer's 9 signal dimensions against 5-day and 20-day forward returns, logs results, and surfaces a simple dashboard table showing each dimension's rolling IC, IC trend (improving/decaying), and a freshness flag if IC has been negative for 2+ consecutive weeks. (2) Z-Score Normalised Signal Composite in signals.py — before compositing the 9 dimension scores into a final signal score, normalise each dimension's score to a Z-score using a rolling 60-day mean and std for that dimension, so dimension scores are cross-sectionally comparable. Both features should have full test coverage. Start with the Z-Score normalisation as it directly impacts every trade decision today, then build the Signal Decay Monitor. Walk me through the current signals.py composite logic first so we can plan the minimal-diff implementation.

untracked_ideas.title: Paper Trading Performance Report — automated weekly P&L and Sharpe summary
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by vision: exit criterion for paper_trading_single_account stage requires demonstrated profitability, but no feature exists to measure or report it systematically
untracked_ideas.rationale: Without an automated weekly performance report showing cumulative P&L, Sharpe ratio, win rate, and drawdown from paper trades, there is no objective basis for deciding when the bot is ready to go live. This is a critical gap in the current stage exit criteria.

untracked_ideas.title: Live vs Paper Trade Shadow Mode — run paper trades in parallel with live to detect execution divergence
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by the live trading transition risk — slippage and fill quality in live trading will differ from paper
untracked_ideas.rationale: Running paper and live simultaneously during the Phase 3 transition allows the team to quantify execution slippage and fill divergence before committing fully to live sizing.

untracked_ideas.title: Capital-Safety Smoke Test — automated pre-flight checklist before any live order session begins
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by the three critical capital-safety bug fixes in recent commits and the Telegram kill switch addition
untracked_ideas.rationale: Given that three capital-safety bugs were found and fixed recently, a pre-flight automated smoke test that validates risk limits, drawdown brake state, connection health, and order sizing before the session starts would prevent regression into dangerous states.

untracked_ideas.title: Feature Flag System for Signal Dimensions — enable/disable individual signal dimensions without code changes
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by Signal Decay Monitor: if a dimension is identified as decayed or harmful, there is currently no clean way to disable it without code changes
untracked_ideas.rationale: A lightweight feature flag per signal dimension (in config.py or a dimension registry) would allow rapid response to decay findings without risky code deploys.

untracked_ideas.title: User Acceptance Testing (UAT) Protocol for Beta Users — structured onboarding test plan
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by multi_user_beta stage: friends and family are non-technical users who need a validated onboarding experience
untracked_ideas.rationale: No spec exists for how beta users will be onboarded, tested, or supported. A lightweight UAT protocol would reduce support burden and catch UX issues before the broader rollout.

untracked_ideas.title: Broker Connection Health Dashboard — real-time IBKR connection status and last-heartbeat display
untracked_ideas.source: Implied by recurring IBKR connection error logs (openTrades() failures, reqGlobalCancel rejections) in test health section
untracked_ideas.rationale: Connection failures to IBKR are a recurring source of bugs. A visible connection health indicator in the dashboard would help operators diagnose issues faster and is a prerequisite for multi-user reliability.

risks.risk: No systematic measure of paper trading profitability exists — the team could keep building features indefinitely without knowing if the bot is actually profitable
risks.mitigation: Implement an automated weekly performance report (P&L, Sharpe, win rate, max drawdown) as a Phase 1 priority. Set a concrete exit criterion: positive Sharpe > 0.5 over 60 trading days before any live capital is risked.

risks.risk: Active feature list has 50 items — WIP explosion risk means developers spread across too many workstreams, nothing ships to production quality
risks.mitigation: The WIP limit commit (max 3 active features) is the right instinct — enforce it strictly. Promote only features that directly contribute to the current stage exit criterion (paper profitability) to active status. Freeze all multi-user and cloud features until Phase 3.

risks.risk: Duplicate feature specs (IC-Weighted Signal Scoring appears twice in Active, Connection Manager and AccountDecisionAgent appear in both Active and Backlog) create ambiguity about what is actually being built
risks.mitigation: Conduct a single deduplication pass across the feature pipeline: merge or close duplicate entries, assign a single canonical spec to each capability, and confirm which version is the active implementation target.

risks.risk: Three critical capital-safety bugs were fixed in orders.py within the last 24 hours — if more exist, they could fire in live trading and cause unintended position sizing or order submission
risks.mitigation: Before advancing to live trading, conduct a dedicated orders.py and risk.py audit session. Add property-based tests that fuzz order sizing and risk limit inputs to surface edge cases. Implement the pre-flight smoke test idea.

risks.risk: bot.py at 2827 lines is the largest module and likely contains mixed concerns (signal generation, order execution, state management) — this creates high coupling and makes bugs harder to isolate
risks.mitigation: Extract Signal Objects from Brain Loop (already a P1 active item) is the highest-leverage refactor. Prioritise it in Phase 1 to reduce bot.py complexity before adding more live trading logic.

risks.risk: Agent JSON parse errors appearing in test logs (agents.py:442) suggest the LLM agent pipeline can return malformed responses in production — if not handled gracefully, this could block trade decisions
risks.mitigation: Verify that the agent fallback path on JSON parse failure is safe (skips the trade rather than crashing or defaulting to a dangerous state). Add a test that explicitly covers malformed LLM response handling and confirms the correct fallback behaviour.

risks.risk: Multi-user credential security is unspecified — IBKR credentials stored in Docker containers without a clear encryption or secrets management design could expose user accounts
risks.mitigation: Before any external user connects their broker account, define and implement a secrets management approach (e.g., Docker secrets, HashiCorp Vault, or encrypted environment variables). The Per-User Docker Compose Containers spec mentions 'encrypted credentials' but no implementation detail exists yet.

risks.risk: Reddit and social sentiment features have mixed research evidence — building multiple Reddit/WSB features risks wasting development cycles on low-alpha signals
risks.mitigation: Do not build Reddit Mention Velocity until Signal Validation with Alphalens confirms it has IC > 0.05 in Decifer's specific universe. Gate all new signal dimensions on Alphalens validation before integration.

roadmap_gaps: Several meaningful gaps exist between the current feature pipeline and the full vision: (1) No paper trading performance reporting capability — there is no feature that measures, tracks, or reports cumulative P&L, Sharpe, and win rate from paper trades, which is the primary exit criterion for the current stage. (2) No pre-flight capital-safety checklist for live trading transitions — given the three critical capital-safety bugs found recently, a systematic pre-flight validation is conspicuously absent. (3) No feature flag or dimension registry system — Signal Decay Monitor will identify bad dimensions but there is no mechanism to disable them without code changes. (4) No UAT or beta user onboarding protocol — the multi_user_beta stage assumes friends and family can onboard themselves but no guided flow or test plan is specified beyond the One-Time Setup Screen. (5) No broker connection health monitoring feature — IBKR connection failures appear repeatedly in logs but no dashboard or alerting capability for connection health exists. (6) No explicit data quality or data freshness monitoring — the data_collector failures in test logs suggest network failures can silently degrade signal inputs, but no data quality gate exists before signals are computed. (7) No rollback or emergency position close procedure beyond the Telegram kill switch — for live and multi-user stages, a documented emergency flatten-all procedure with automated confirmation is needed. (8) The HMM Regime Detection feature appears in both Active (D P2) and Backlog (D P2) with no resolution — the regime detection capability is critical for Phase 1 signal routing but its implementation status is ambiguous.
